# MWS Market Context — AUTO-GENERATED 2026-03-13

## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

The Fed's March 18 meeting sees nearly 100% probability of no rate change. Oil prices surging amid U.S.-Iran war tensions have caused traders to push back rate-cut expectations. Core PCE inflation expected at 3.1% year-over-year for January, up from December and further from the Fed's 2% target, with inflation pressures evident before Iran strike.

February CPI showed 2.4% annual rise, but energy spike from Iran conflict expected to push March top-line inflation toward 3%. 10-2 yield spread at 0.57% on March 11, positive curve.

**Materiality: HIGH**
**Signal: CONTRADICTS all sleeves**  
The shift from expected rate cuts to prolonged pause contradicts the bullish monetary conditions backdrop priced into current momentum. Real rates rising despite nominal stability creates headwind for gold (opposite traditional relationship) and compresses equity valuations. Iran war premium embeds stagflation risk—bad for growth sleeves, ambiguous for monetary hedges given competing inflation/real-rate dynamics.

---

## 2. Geopolitical / Commodity

Brent crude settled at $94/bbl on March 9, up 50% from year start, highest since September 2023, driven by Strait of Hormuz blockade and Middle East production shut-ins. Nearly 20 mb/d of crude and product exports disrupted with limited bypass options. Brent vaulted over $100, surging to $111.45 two-year high following blockade confirmation.

VIX spiked amid Iran war volatility; index showing elevated geopolitical fear. VIX opened at 27.85 (current reading above 25 = market turbulence threshold).

**Materiality: HIGH**  
**Signal: CONFIRMS defense_energy, CONTRADICTS broad equity**  
Hormuz closure is unprecedented supply shock—pro-cyclical energy equities benefit near-term but macro spillover (inflation shock, recession risk) threatens broader equity rally. XLE/ITA direct beneficiaries. Gold/silver ambiguous: crisis bid vs. real-rate pressure from Fed hawkishness tug-of-war.

---

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

U.S. regulators drafted rules requiring government approval to ship AI chips anywhere outside U.S., giving government role in every export sale. Trump administration eased H200 chip export controls to China from presumption-of-denial to case-by-case review in January 2026, but broader worldwide export control framework proposed March 2026.

Trump FTC advanced broad antitrust probe into Microsoft covering cloud, AI, and software. No major AI-specific approval/rejection news this week.

**Materiality: MEDIUM**  
**Signal: CONFIRMS ai_tech (regulatory clarity emerging, though restrictive)**  
Export control framework formalization reduces uncertainty for NVIDIA/AMD but creates long-term friction for hyperscaler buildouts. AI demand structural thesis intact—near-term momentum not materially changed. Watch for implementation details.

---

## 4. Energy / Materials

Covered in Section 2 (geopolitical). Additional:

NRC authorized TerraPower construction permit for Kemmerer advanced reactor—first non-light-water reactor approval in 40 years, technical review completed in 18 months. $800 million funding for small modular reactors awarded December 2025, uranium enrichment investment $2.7 billion January 2026.

NexGen's Rook I uranium project received final Canadian regulatory approval March 5, construction starts summer 2026. Rook I capable of 30 million lbs/year, 20% of current global uranium supply.

China rare earth price index rose 11.84% from Dec 31 to Jan 26; praseodymium-neodymium oxide surged 29%.

**Materiality: HIGH**  
**Signal: CONFIRMS strategic_materials, defense_energy**  
Nuclear licensing acceleration and uranium project approvals validate URNM thesis. Rare earth price surge (driven by magnet demand for EVs/wind) directly supports REMX. Copper/lithium not mentioned this week but benefit from same electrification trend. Strategic materials momentum structurally intact.

## 5. Crypto / Regulatory

SEC-CFTC Memorandum of Understanding executed March 11, classifying BTC and ETH as commodities under CFTC, tokens from ICOs under SEC securities oversight. U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded first two consecutive weeks of inflows since October 2025, attracting $568.45M, coinciding with MOU announcement.

IBIT pulled $115M inflows March 11; BlackRock launched ETHB (staked Ethereum ETF) March 12. GENIUS Act passage mid-2025 enabled staking products.

**Materiality: MEDIUM**  
**Signal: CONFIRMS crypto (regulatory clarity milestone)**  
SEC-CFTC jurisdictional clarity removes years of overhang. ETF inflows returning despite "extreme fear" retail sentiment (Fear/Greed at 15) suggests institutional reassessment. IBIT dormant in portfolio—regulatory tailwind now present but momentum signal still negative. No change to current zero allocation yet justified.

## 6. Biotech / FDA

Rocket Pharma's Kresladi (LAD-I gene therapy) could be approved by March 28 after 2024 manufacturing rejection. Rhythm Pharma's Imcivree March 20 decision deadline for additional clearance. Three recent FDA rejections (including bitopertin despite priority voucher) signal unpredictability under new leadership.

FDA approved 55 new treatments in 2025, down from 60+ in 2023.

**Materiality: LOW**  
**Signal: NEUTRAL for biotech**  
Approval pipeline intact but pace/predictability down. XBI exposure reflects sector beta, not single-drug risk. No change to thesis.

## 7. Rates / Currency

Covered in Section 1. Addendum:

10-year Treasury yield fell 0.25pp in two weeks (steepest since March 2025) to late-October levels; inflation-protected TIPS yields at same level when gold was breaking $4000 for first time, yet gold doesn't respond to falling yields as historically expected.

Real yield on 10-year TIPS averaged 1.97% in 2025, highest since 2007, yet gold rose 65%. Gold-TIPS correlation shifted from -0.73 (2003-2021) to +0.02 (2022-present).

**Materiality: HIGH**  
**Signal: CONFIRMS precious_metals (structural regime shift)**  
Historical gold-real rate relationship broken since 2022 Ukraine invasion. Current rally driven by central bank buying, de-dollarization, and crisis hedging—not rate sensitivity. Validates IAUM/SIVR momentum despite "unfavorable" rate backdrop. Structural, not cyclical.
